codeSystems.description: This is the code specifying the high-level kind of document (e.g. Prescription, Discharge Summary, Report, etc.). Note: Class code for documents comes from LOINC, and is based upon one of the following:The type of service described by the document. It is described at a very high level in Section 7.3 of the LOINC Manual. The type study performed. It was determined by identifying modalities for study reports. The section of the chart where the document is placed. It was determined from the SETs created for Claims Attachment requests.

codeSystems.name: DocumentReferenceFormatCodeSet
codeSystems.title: DocumentReference Format Code Set
codeSystems.description: The value set is defined to be the set of format codes defined by the IHE Technical Framework, and also including additional format codes defined by the    HL7. The value set is listed in HITSP C80 Table 2-153 Format Code Value Set Definition,    with additions published later by IHE as published    at http://wiki.ihe.net/index.php?title=IHE_Format_Codes   and with additions published later by HL7 as published at https://confluence.hl7.org/display/SD/Format+Codes+for+IHE+XDS.   This is the code specifying the technical format of the document. Along with the typeCode,    it should provide sufficient information to allow any potential document consumer to know    if it will be able to process the document. The code shall be sufficiently specific to    ensure processing/display by identifying a document encoding, structure and template. The actual list of codes here is incomplete

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationFunctionCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Function Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the function a practitioner or organization may play in the immunization event. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationFundingSource
codeSystems.title: Immunization Funding Source
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the vaccine administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationOriginCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Origin Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the data when the report of the immunization event is not based on information from the person, entity or organization who administered the vaccine. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationProgramEligibility
codeSystems.title: Immunization Program Eligibility
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the patient's eligibility for a vaccination program. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose of vaccine was administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRouteCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Route Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the administrative routes used during vaccination. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: CodesForImmunizationSiteOfAdministration
codeSystems.title: Codes for Immunization Site of Administration
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the body site where the vaccination occurred. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Status Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the current status of the administered dose of vaccine.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationSubpotentReason
codeSystems.title: Immunization Subpotent Reason
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose is considered to be subpotent. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationTargetDiseaseCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Target Disease Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the disease that the dose is being administered against. This value set is provided as a suggestive example and includes the SNOMED CT concepts from the 64572001 (Disease) hierarchy.

codeSystems.name: ObservationInterpretationCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Interpretation Codes
codeSystems.description:
A categorical assessment, providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation value,    such as “normal”/ “abnormal”,”low” / “high”, “better” / “worse”, “susceptible” / “resistant”, “expected”/ “not expected”.    The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.   
   
   Notes:
   
   This is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) in R1 (Representative Realm) and in FHIR in    Observation.interpretation. Historically these values come from the laboratory domain, and these codes are extensively    used. The value set binding is extensible, so codes outside the value set that are needed for interpretation concepts    (i.e. particular meanings) that are not included in the value set can be used, and these new codes may also be added to    the value set and published in a future version.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser Observation

codeSystems.name: DetailedEthnicity

codeSystems.description: The codes for the concepts 'Unknown', 'Asked but no answer', 'other, and the 41 [CDC ethnicity codes](http://www.cdc.gov/phin/resources/vocabulary/index.html) that are grouped under one of the 2 OMB ethnicity category codes.

codeSystems.name: DetailedRace
codeSystems.title: Detailed Race
codeSystems.description: The codes for the concepts 'Unknown', 'Asked but no answer', 'other, and the 900+ [CDC Race codes](http://www.cdc.gov/phin/resources/vocabulary/index.html) that are grouped under one of the 5 OMB race category codes.

codeSystems.name: OmbEthnicityCategories
codeSystems.title: OMB Ethnicity Categories
codeSystems.description: The codes for the concepts 'Unknown', 'Asked but no answer', and the ethnicity categories - 'Hispanic or Latino' and 'Not Hispanic or Latino' - as defined by the [OMB Standards for Maintaining, Collecting, and Presenting Federal Data on Race and Ethnicity, Statistical Policy Directive No. 15, as revised, October 30, 1997](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/FR-1997-10-30/pdf/97-28653.pdf).

codeSystems.name: OmbRaceCategories
codeSystems.title: OMB Race Categories
codeSystems.description:
The codes for the concepts 'Unknown', 'Asked but Unknown', 'Other Race'* and
the codes for the five race categories - 'American Indian' or 'Alaska Native',
'Asian', 'Black or African American', 'Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific
Islander', and 'White' - as defined by the [OMB Standards for Maintaining,
Collecting, and Presenting Federal Data on Race and Ethnicity, Statistical
Policy Directive No. 15, as revised, October 30,
1997](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/FR-1997-10-30/pdf/97-28653.pdf)

\* The use of 'Other Race' is discouraged for statistical analysis.

codeSystems.name: LanguageCodesWithLanguageAndOptionallyARegionModifier
codeSystems.title: Language codes with language and optionally a region modifier
codeSystems.description: This value set includes codes from [BCP-47](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). This value set matches the ONC 2015 Edition LanguageCommunication data element value set within C-CDA to use a 2 character language code if one exists,   and a 3 character code if a 2 character code does not exist. It points back to [RFC 5646](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5646), however only the language codes are required, all other elements are optional.

codeSystems.name: USCoreConditionCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Condition Codes
codeSystems.description: This describes the problem. Diagnosis/Problem List is broadly defined as a series of brief statements that catalog a patient's medical, nursing, dental, social, preventative and psychiatric events and issues that are relevant to that patient's healthcare (e.g., signs, symptoms, and defined conditions). ICD-10 is appropriate for Diagnosis information, and ICD-9 for historical information.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDiagnosticReportLabCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Diagnostic Report Laboratory Codes
codeSystems.description: The Document Type value set includes all LOINC  values whose CLASSTYPE is LABORATORY in the LOINC database
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser DiagnosticReport

codeSystems.name: USCoreNonLaboratoryCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Non Laboratory Codes
codeSystems.description: Non laboratory LOINC concepts such as codes for observations like vital signs, hemodynamics, intake/output, EKG, obstetric ultrasound, cardiac echo and other clinical observations. The codes selected should represent discrete and narrative diagnostic observations and reports

codeSystems.name: USCoreDischargeDisposition
codeSystems.title: US Core Discharge Disposition
codeSystems.description: [National Uniform Billing Committee](http://www.nubc.org/), manual UB-04, UB form locator 17.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDocumentReferenceCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core DocumentReference Category
codeSystems.description: The US Core DocumentReferences Category Value Set is a 'starter set' of categories supported for fetching and storing clinical notes.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDocumentReferenceType
codeSystems.title: US Core DocumentReference Type
codeSystems.description: The US Core DocumentReference Type Value Set includes all LOINC  values whose SCALE is DOC in the LOINC database and the HL7 v3 Code System NullFlavor concept 'unknown'

codeSystems.name: USCoreEncounterType
codeSystems.title: US Core Encounter Type
codeSystems.description:
The type of encounter: a specific code indicating type of service provided. This value set includes codes from SNOMED CT decending from the concept 308335008 (Patient encounter procedure (procedure)) and codes from the Current Procedure and Terminology (CPT) found in the following CPT sections:
- 99201-99499 E/M - 99500-99600 home health (mainly nonphysician, such as newborn care in home) - 99605-99607 medication management - 98966-98968 non physician telephone services
(subscription to AMA Required)

codeSystems.name: USCoreObservationSmokingStatusStatus
codeSystems.title: US Core Status for Smoking Status Observation
codeSystems.description: Codes providing the status of an observation for smoking status. Constrained to `final`and `entered-in-error`.

codeSystems.name: USCoreProblemOrHealthConcern
codeSystems.title: US Core Problem or Health Concern
codeSystems.description: Code set for category codes for *US Core Condition Problems and Health Concerns Profile* consisting of the concepts "problem" and "health-concern".

codeSystems.name: USCoreProcedureCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Procedure Codes
codeSystems.description: Concepts from CPT, SNOMED CT, HCPCS Level II Alphanumeric Codes, ICD-10-PCS,CDT and LOINC code systems that can be used to indicate the type of procedure performed.

codeSystems.name: USCoreProviderRoleNucc
codeSystems.title: US Core Provider Role (NUCC)
codeSystems.description: Provider roles codes which are composed of the NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set classification codes for providers. Only concepts with a classification and no specialization are included.

codeSystems.name: USCoreSmokingStatusObservationCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Smoking Status Observation Codes
codeSystems.description: The US Core Smoking Status Observation Codes Value Set is a 'starter set' of concepts to capture smoking status.

codeSystems.name: UspsTwoLetterAlphabeticCodes
codeSystems.title: USPS Two Letter Alphabetic Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set defines two letter USPS alphabetic codes.

codeSystems.name: UsCoreVitalSigns
codeSystems.title: US Core Vital Signs ValueSet
codeSystems.description: The vital sign codes from the base FHIR and US Core Vital Signs.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSReAdmissionIndicator
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-re-admissionIndicator
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes which are used to specify that a patient is being re-admitted to a healthcare facility from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSBedStatus
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-bedStatus
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify the state of a bed in an inpatient setting, and is used to determine if a patient may be assigned to it or not.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAppointmentReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-appointmentReasonCodes
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that describe the kind of appointment or the reason why an appointment has been scheduled.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAdditivePreservative
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-additivePreservative
codeSystems.description: Concepts specifying any additive introduced to the specimen before or at the time of collection.  These additives may be introduced in order to preserve, maintain or enhance the particular nature or component of the specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser Specimen

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSSpecimenType
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-specimenType
codeSystems.description: Concepts that describe the precise nature of an entity that may be used as the source material for an observation.  This is one of two code systems that are used instead of table 0070 (code system xxxx) which conflated specimen types and specimen collection methods.   Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser Specimen

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSSpecimenCondition
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-specimenCondition
codeSystems.description: Concepts of modes or states of being that describe the nature of a specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser Specimen

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSRelevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-relevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify additional clinical information about the patient or specimen to report the supporting and/or suspected diagnosis and clinical findings on requests for interpreted diagnostic studies.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-LabObservationChemhe: Lab Observation: Chem, hem, tox, ria, ser Specimen

codeSystems.name: v2.0360.2.7
codeSystems.title: v2 table 0360, Version 2.7
codeSystems.description: FHIR Value set/code system definition for HL7 v2 table 0360 ver 2.9 ( Degree/License/Certificate)

codeSystems.name: ActCode
codeSystems.title: v3 Code System ActCode
codeSystems.description:
A code specifying the particular kind of Act that the Act-instance represents within its class.  Constraints:
The kind of Act (e.g. physical examination, serum potassium, inpatient encounter, charge financial transaction, etc.) is specified with a code from one of several, typically external, coding systems.  The coding system will depend on the class of Act, such as LOINC for observations, etc. Conceptually, the Act.code must be a specialization of the Act.classCode. This is why the structure of ActClass domain should be reflected in the superstructure of the ActCode domain and then individual codes or externally referenced vocabularies subordinated under these domains that reflect the ActClass structure. Act.classCode and Act.code are not modifiers of each other but the Act.code concept should really imply the Act.classCode concept. For a negative example, it is not appropriate to use an Act.code "potassium" together with and Act.classCode for "laboratory observation" to somehow mean "potassium laboratory observation" and then use the same Act.code for "potassium" together with Act.classCode for "medication" to mean "substitution of potassium". This mutually modifying use of Act.code and Act.classCode is not permitted.

codeSystems.name: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.title: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.description: Domain provides codes that qualify the ActEncounterClass (ENC)

codeSystems.name: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.title: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.description: Identifies types of dispensing events

codeSystems.name: ActPriority
codeSystems.title: ActPriority
codeSystems.description:
A code or set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency,) specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.

*Discussion:* This attribute is used in orders to indicate the ordered priority, and in event documentation it indicates the actual priority used to perform the act. In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.

codeSystems.name: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.title: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.description: A role of a place that further classifies the setting (e.g., accident site, road side, work site, community location) in which services are delivered.
